searchDocs.title: MovementState​
searchDocs.content: General </> type MovementState = { Character: Model? , Humanoid: Humanoid? , RootPart: BasePart? , CameraLookVector: Vector3? , OnGround: boolean?, GroundNormal: Vector3? , [string]: any } Per-player movement state updated each simulation step. Built-in fields track the character and ground info; modules add custom fields through NeededStates.

searchDocs.title: BindingProps​
searchDocs.content: Input Action System </> type BindingProps = { Backward: Enum.KeyCode?, ClampMagnitudeToOne: boolean?, Down: Enum.KeyCode?, Forward: Enum.KeyCode?, KeyCode: Enum.KeyCode?, Left: Enum.KeyCode?, PointerIndex: number?, PressedThreshold: number?, PrimaryModifier: Enum.KeyCode?, ReleasedThreshold: number?, ResponseCurve: number?, Right: Enum.KeyCode?, Scale: number?, SecondaryModifier: Enum.KeyCode?, UIButton: Enum.UserInputType?, Up: Enum.KeyCode?, Vector2Scale: Vector2? , Vector3Scale: Vector3? } Optional input binding settings for an action. Keys map to Roblox InputBinding properties such as key codes, modifiers, and scale values.

searchDocs.title: ActionDefinition​
searchDocs.content: Input Action System </> type ActionDefinition = { Name: string, Type: Enum.InputActionType, Bindings: {[string]: BindingProps}, ApplyInput: ( state: MovementState, inputState: any ) → () } Describes one input action in a movement module. ApplyInput writes the action state into the player's movement state each simulation step.

searchDocs.title: MovementModuleDefinition​
searchDocs.content: General </> type MovementModuleDefinition = { Name: string, Priority: number?, Actions: {ActionDefinition}, NeededStates: NeededStatesDefaults, Process: ( state: MovementState, deltaTime: number ) → (), Disable: {string}? } Describes a movement module: input actions, default state, per-frame logic, and optional Roblox actions to disable. Lower Priority runs first.

searchDocs.title: PreCameraSimulationEntry​
searchDocs.content: Callbacks </> type PreCameraSimulationEntry = { name: string, callback: PreCameraSimulationCallback } Internal entry for a registered pre-camera simulation callback.

searchDocs.title: PostCameraVisualEntry​
searchDocs.content: Callbacks </> type PostCameraVisualEntry = { name: string, callback: PostCameraVisualCallback, priority: number } Internal entry for a registered post-camera visual callback.

searchDocs.title: AnimationSettings​
searchDocs.content: General </> type AnimationSettings = { Speed: number, Weight: number, FadeTime: number, StopFadeTime: number, Looped: boolean, Priority: Enum.AnimationPriority, Animation: Animation } Playback settings for a registered animation.

searchDocs.title: RegisterDefinition​
searchDocs.content: Register </> ServerAuthToolkit.RegisterDefinition( self: ServerAuthToolkit, definition: ServerAuthToolkitTypes.MovementModuleDefinition-- Module definition to register ) → () Registers a movement module with input actions, default state fields, and aProcess function. Modules run in priority order (lower runs first). Register before Start when possible; if already started, active players are updated immediately. Duplicate names are ignored.

searchDocs.title: Teleport​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server Utility </> ServerAuthToolkit.Teleport( self: ServerAuthToolkit, player: Player ,-- The player whose character to teleport targetCFrame: CFrame ,-- Destination CFrame; position is used, facing is flattened modifyState: ((state: MovementState) → ())?,-- Optional hook to mutate the movement state before replication preserveLookDirection: boolean?-- When true, keeps the current facing instead of using targetCFrame ) → boolean-- True if the teleport was applied Teleports a player's character to a target CFrame with server authority. Server-only. Sets the root part CFrame and replicates the new state so predicting clients roll back to it. The toolkit does not assume any module-specific state fields. PassmodifyState to mutate the player's authoritative movement state before it is captured to attributes (for example, zeroing a module's Velocity so the character does not keep its momentum after the move). The callback runs after the position is resolved and before replication.

searchDocs.title: RegisterPreCameraSimulation​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client Camera </> ServerAuthToolkit.RegisterPreCameraSimulation( self: ServerAuthToolkit, callbackName: string,-- Unique name for this callback callback: ServerAuthToolkitTypes.PreCameraSimulationCallback-- Called each pre-simulation step with deltaTime ) → () → ()-- Unregisters the callback Registers a callback that runs on the client before movement simulation each frame. Use for client-only setup that must happen before input is applied. Callbacks run in registration order. Server calls log a warning and return a no-op unregister function.

searchDocs.title: RegisterPostCameraVisualEffect​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client Camera </> ServerAuthToolkit.RegisterPostCameraVisualEffect( self: ServerAuthToolkit, callbackName: string,-- Unique name for this callback callback: ServerAuthToolkitTypes.PostCameraVisualCallback,-- Called each render step with deltaTime renderPriorityOffset: number?-- Offset from camera render priority. Defaults to 2 ) → () → ()-- Unregisters the callback Registers a visual callback that runs on the client after the camera updates. Use for camera shake, FOV changes, or other effects that should not affect movement simulation. Server calls log a warning and return a no-op unregister function.

searchDocs.title: GetStoredSimulationCameraBaseCFrame​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client Camera </> ServerAuthToolkit.GetStoredSimulationCameraBaseCFrame(self: ServerAuthToolkit) → CFrame? -- The stored simulation camera CFrame Returns the camera CFrame captured for client movement simulation. The toolkit stores this after the camera updates so simulation uses a stable camera state. Returns nil on the server or before a capture exists.

searchDocs.title: ResetNeededStates​
searchDocs.content: States </> ServerAuthToolkit.ResetNeededStates( self: ServerAuthToolkit, playerMovementState: ServerAuthToolkitTypes.MovementState-- The movement state to reset ) → () Resets a player's custom movement state fields to the merged defaults from all registered modules. Only fields from module NeededStates are reset. Built-in fields like Character and RootPart are not changed.

searchDocs.title: GetLocalMovementState​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client States </> ServerAuthToolkit.GetLocalMovementState(self: ServerAuthToolkit) → ServerAuthToolkitTypes.MovementState?-- The local player's movement state Returns the local player's movement state table. This is the same table updated each simulation step by registered modules. Returns nil on the server.

searchDocs.title: RegisterAnimations​
searchDocs.content: Register </> ServerAuthToolkit.RegisterAnimations( self: ServerAuthToolkit, animations: {[string]: ServerAuthToolkitTypes.AnimationSettings}-- Animation names to settings ) → () Registers animations for use with the animation API. Each name maps to playback settings used by PlayAnimation, StopAnimation, and the animation query methods.

searchDocs.title: PlayAnimation​
searchDocs.content: Animations </> ServerAuthToolkit.PlayAnimation( self: ServerAuthToolkit, animationName: string,-- The animation to play speedOverride: number?-- Optional playback speed. Uses registered speed if omitted ) → () Plays a registered animation. The animation must be registered first with RegisterAnimations.

searchDocs.title: StopAnimation​
searchDocs.content: Animations </> ServerAuthToolkit.StopAnimation( self: ServerAuthToolkit, animationName: string-- The animation to stop ) → () Stops a registered animation. Uses the registered stop fade time when blending out.

searchDocs.title: IsAnimationPlaying​
searchDocs.content: Animations </> ServerAuthToolkit.IsAnimationPlaying( self: ServerAuthToolkit, animationName: string-- The animation to check ) → boolean-- True if the animation is playing Returns whether a registered animation is currently playing.

searchDocs.title: IsAnimationFinished​
searchDocs.content: Animations </> ServerAuthToolkit.IsAnimationFinished( self: ServerAuthToolkit, animationName: string-- The animation to check ) → boolean-- True if the animation has finished Returns whether a non-looped animation has finished playing. Looped animations always return false.

searchDocs.title: WriteFlatLookDirection​
searchDocs.content: Utility </> ServerAuthToolkit.WriteFlatLookDirection( self: ServerAuthToolkit, rootPart: BasePart ,-- The character root part flatLookDirection: Vector3 -- The horizontal look direction in world space ) → () Stores a flat look direction on a root part for replication. The toolkit reads this at the end of each simulation step to orient the character. The Y component should usually be zero.

searchDocs.title: ReadFlatLookDirection​
searchDocs.content: Utility </> ServerAuthToolkit.ReadFlatLookDirection( self: ServerAuthToolkit, rootPart: BasePart -- The character root part ) → Vector3 -- The stored flat look direction Reads the flat look direction stored on a root part. Returns Vector3.new(0, 0, -1) if no direction has been stored.

searchDocs.title: QuantizeFlatDirection​
searchDocs.content: Utility </> ServerAuthToolkit.QuantizeFlatDirection( self: ServerAuthToolkit, flatDirection: Vector3 -- The direction to quantize ) → Vector3 -- The quantized direction on the horizontal plane Rounds a flat direction to a fixed yaw step for stable network replication. Yaw is quantized to 0.25 degree steps. Useful before callingWriteFlatLookDirection.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: Register </> ServerAuthToolkit.Start(self: ServerAuthToolkit) → () Starts movement simulation, player lifecycle handling, and the animation helper. Call once on the server and once on the client after registering modules. Safe to call multiple times; later calls do nothing.
